overview: Develops listening, speaking, reading, vocabulary, grammar, and academic writing skills for professional communication.
recommendedBooks: V. Sasikumar et al., A Course in Listening and Speaking I, Cambridge University Press, 2006
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use, Cambridge University Press, 2019
Alice Oshima and Ann Hogue, Introduction to Academic Writing, Pearson Longman, 2007

overview: Introduces data formats, cleaning, wrangling, descriptive statistics, spreadsheets, visualization, pivot tables, curve fitting, and regression.
recommendedBooks: Wayne Winston, Microsoft Excel Data Analysis and Business Modeling, Pearson, 2022
Michael Alexander, Richard Kusleika, John Walkenbach, Excel 2019 Bible, Wiley, 2018

overview: Covers probability, random variables, probability functions, expectations, moments, multivariate distributions, covariance, correlation, and limit theorems.
recommendedBooks: Hogg, McKean and Craig, Introduction to Mathematical Statistics, Pearson, 2012
Rice, Mathematical Statistics and Data Analysis, Cengage, 2006

overview: Builds foundations in C programming, data types, operators, control flow, functions, recursion, arrays, pointers, structures, input/output, and files.
recommendedBooks: Kernighan and Ritchie, The C Programming Language, Pearson, 2015
Byron S. Gottfried, Programming with C, McGraw Hill, 2018

overview: Studies systems of equations, matrices, vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, inner products, QR factorization, SVD, and matrix calculus.
recommendedBooks: David Poole, Linear Algebra: A Modern Introduction, Cengage, 2014
Seymour Lipschutz and Marc Lars Lipson, Linear Algebra, McGraw Hill, 2009

overview: Introduces the data science workflow from collection and curation to cleaning, exploration, model building, validation, evaluation, and applications.
recommendedBooks: Rafael A. Irizarry, Introduction to Data Science, CRC Press, 2019
Hui Lin and Ming Li, Practitioner's Guide to Data Science, Chapman & Hall/CRC, 2023

overview: Explains computer architecture, CPU/GPU basics, operating systems, file systems, shell programming, device drivers, system monitoring, package management, and user administration.
recommendedBooks: Joan Lambert and Steve Lambert, Windows 10 Step by Step, Microsoft Press, 2015
Mark Sobell and Matthew Helmke, A Practical Guide to Linux Commands, Editors, and Shell Programming, Addison-Wesley, 2017

overview: Covers Python variables, data types, control flow, lists, dictionaries, sets, functions, recursion, classes, exceptions, and file handling.
recommendedBooks: Charles Dierbach, Introduction to Computer Science using Python, Wiley, 2015
Mark Lutz, Learning Python, O'Reilly Media, 2013
Wes McKinney, Python for Data Analysis, O'Reilly Media, 2012

overview: Surveys artificial intelligence, intelligent agents, search, logic, knowledge representation, planning, uncertainty, supervised learning, unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning, and applications.
recommendedBooks: Russell and Norvig, Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach, Pearson, 2020
Sutton and Barto, Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, MIT Press, 2018

overview: Builds algorithmic data-structure foundations with arrays, stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, heaps, sorting, searching, balanced trees, graphs, BFS, DFS, and hashing.
recommendedBooks: Goodrich, Tamassia and Goldwasser, Data Structures and Algorithms in Python, Wiley, 2013
Mark Allen Weiss, Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in C++, Pearson, 2014

overview: Covers asymptotic analysis, sorting, searching, greedy methods, divide and conquer, dynamic programming, amortized analysis, graph traversal, MST, shortest paths, and max flow.
recommendedBooks: Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest and Stein, Introduction to Algorithms, PHI, 2009
Aho, Hopcroft and Ullman, The Design and Analysis of Computer Algorithms, Pearson, 2006

overview: Introduces R programming, vectors, matrices, operators, missing-data handling, conditionals, loops, functions, lists, factors, strings, data frames, file import/export, and visualization.
recommendedBooks: Lafaye de Micheaux, Drouilhet and Liquet, The R Software, Springer, 2013
Zuur, Ieno and Meesters, A Beginner's Guide to R, Springer, 2009
Heumann, Schomaker and Shalabh, Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis with R, Springer, 2016

overview: Studies relational databases, ER models, relational algebra, SQL, constraints, triggers, storage, indexing, transactions, concurrency, recovery, normalization, administration, connectivity, and NoSQL.
recommendedBooks: Ramakrishnan and Gehrke, Database Management Systems, McGraw Hill, 2014
Elmasri and Navathe, Fundamentals of Database Systems, Pearson, 2017
Garcia-Molina, Ullman and Widom, Database Systems: The Complete Book, Pearson, 2013

overview: Builds Java programming skills across syntax, data types, control flow, methods, object-oriented concepts, exception handling, collections, files, data structures, GUI, database connectivity, multithreading, networking, and AI libraries.
recommendedBooks: Herbert Schildt, Java: The Complete Reference, McGraw-Hill, 2019
David Kopec, Classic Computer Science Problems in Java, Manning, 2021

overview: Covers optimization problem formulation, convex sets and functions, unconstrained methods, gradient method, Newton and quasi-Newton methods, simplex, duality, constrained methods, and convex optimization.
recommendedBooks: Chong and Zak, An Introduction to Optimization, Wiley India, 2017
Boyd and Vandenberghe, Convex Optimization, Cambridge, 2004

overview: Reviews random variables and statistical inference for economics, then develops linear regression, least squares, Gauss-Markov theorem, hypothesis testing, model fit, nonlinear models, dummy variables, heteroscedasticity, autocorrelation, multicollinearity, omitted variables, instrumental variables, and binary response models.
recommendedBooks: Jeffrey M. Wooldridge, Introductory Econometrics: A Modern Approach, South-Western, 2020
Davidson and MacKinnon, Econometric Theory and Methods, Oxford, 2009

overview: Explores data mining and warehousing through dimensional modeling, ETL, similarity, locality-sensitive hashing, frequent pattern mining, graph mining, clustering, and data-stream mining.
recommendedBooks: Han, Pei and Tong, Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques, Morgan Kaufmann, 2022
Robinson, Webber and Eifrem, Graph Databases, O'Reilly, 2015
Ponniah, Data Warehousing Fundamentals, Wiley, 2012

overview: Develops statistical inference through point estimation, properties of estimators, MLE, Fisher information, confidence intervals, bootstrap, hypothesis testing, likelihood ratio tests, Neyman-Pearson lemma, Bayesian inference, MAP, and Bayesian least squares.
recommendedBooks: Larry Wasserman, All of Statistics, Springer, 2004
Pishro-Nik, Introduction to Probability, Statistics, and Random Processes, Kappa Research, 2014
Bertsekas and Tsitsiklis, Introduction to Probability, Athena Scientific, 2008

overview: Introduces continuous-time and discrete-time signals, signal operations, signal spaces, Fourier series, Fourier transform, sampling, discrete-time Fourier transform, DFT, system properties, LTI systems, impulse response, convolution, and frequency response.
recommendedBooks: B. P. Lathi, Principles of Linear Systems and Signals, Oxford, 2009
H. Hsu and R. Rajan, Signals and Systems, Schaum Series, 2017

overview: Covers social media content, crawling ethics and APIs, text analysis, tokenization, lemmatization, retrieval, social graph analytics, influence, ranking, and applied social media analytics.
recommendedBooks: Manning, Raghavan and Schutze, Introduction to Information Retrieval, Cambridge, 2008
Subodha Kumar and Liangfei Qiu, Social Media Analytics and Practical Applications

overview: Focuses on dataset structure, data models, data preparation, visual perception, visualization guidelines, coordinate systems, dashboard design, visual encodings, and effective data storytelling.
recommendedBooks: Harry J. Foxwell, Creating Good Data, Apress, 2020
Claus O. Wilke, Fundamentals of Data Visualization, O'Reilly, 2019

overview: Introduces time-series data, trends, differencing, stationary processes, linear time-series models, estimation, diagnostics, smoothing, ARIMA-style modeling, and practical forecasting.
recommendedBooks: Brockwell and Davis, Introduction to Time Series and Forecasting, Springer, 2002
Hyndman and Athanasopoulos, Forecasting: Principles and Practice

overview: Builds core machine-learning concepts across supervised and unsupervised learning, generative and discriminative models, feature vectors, Gaussian distributions, Bayesian classification, regression, clustering, and model evaluation.
recommendedBooks: Ethem Alpaydin, Introduction to Machine Learning, Prentice Hall, 2015
Duda, Hart and Stork, Pattern Classification, Wiley, 2006

overview: Studies recommender-system techniques including nearest-neighbor methods, association rules, content-based filtering, collaborative filtering, matrix factorization, SVD, alternating least squares, ranking, and evaluation.
recommendedBooks: Charu C. Aggarwal, Recommender Systems: The Textbook, Springer, 2016
Jannach, Zanker and Felfernig, Recommender Systems: An Introduction, Cambridge, 2011

overview: Covers cloud fundamentals, distributed systems, service-oriented and event-driven architecture, SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, virtualization, deployment, storage, security, monitoring, and cloud-native design.
recommendedBooks: N. B. Ruparelia, Cloud Computing, MIT Press, 2023
Thomas Erl and Eric Barcelo Monroy, Cloud Computing: Concepts, Technology, Security and Architecture, Pearson, 2024

overview: Introduces deep learning concepts such as multilayer perceptrons, backpropagation, activations, losses, gradient methods, CNNs, regularization, sequence models, and deployment considerations.
recommendedBooks: Goodfellow, Bengio and Courville, Deep Learning, MIT Press, 2016
Michael Nielsen, Neural Networks and Deep Learning, 2015

overview: Connects wireless communication foundations with supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement-learning, and deep-learning techniques for communication systems.
recommendedBooks: Textbooks to be updated in official syllabus

overview: Studies time value of money, bond valuation, yield, duration, convexity, equity valuation, portfolio risk and return, diversification, and portfolio analytics.
recommendedBooks: Textbooks to be updated in official syllabus

overview: Develops responsible AI judgment through fairness metrics, bias mitigation, explainability, privacy, surveillance, autonomous systems, accountability, global governance, and generative AI ethics.
recommendedBooks: Barocas, Hardt and Narayanan, Fairness and Machine Learning
Additional references to be updated in official syllabus

overview: Applies deep learning to computer vision through image-processing review, CNNs, encoder-decoder models, detection, recognition, segmentation, inpainting, super-resolution, depth estimation, and vision applications.
recommendedBooks: Richard Szeliski, Computer Vision: Algorithms and Applications, Springer, 2022
Goodfellow, Bengio and Courville, Deep Learning, MIT Press, 2016

overview: Explores deep-learning systems with automatic differentiation, neural-network libraries, parallel programming, hardware parallelism, CPU/GPU/microcontroller tradeoffs, quantization, pruning, and efficient deployment.
recommendedBooks: Andres Rodriguez, Deep Learning Systems, Morgan & Claypool, 2021
Simon J. D. Prince, Understanding Deep Learning

overview: Connects digital manufacturing and data science through 3D printing technologies, process planning, additive manufacturing data, monitoring, quality control, optimization, and AI-enabled production.
recommendedBooks: L. Jyothish Kumar, Pulak M. Pandey and David Ian Wimpenny, 3D Printing and Additive Manufacturing Technologies, Springer, 2019
Ian Gibson, David Rosen and Brent Stucker, Additive Manufacturing Technologies

overview: Builds leadership capability through leadership theory, emotional intelligence, leadership styles, leader-member exchange, change and crisis leadership, conflict management, feedback, persuasion, and motivation.
recommendedBooks: Northouse, Leadership: Theory and Practice, Sage, 2022
Yukl, Leadership in Organizations, Pearson, 2020
Kouzes and Posner, The Leadership Challenge, Wiley, 2017

overview: Provides hands-on LLM application engineering with tokens, context windows, inference APIs, prompt engineering, structured outputs, tool calling, guardrails, LangChain workflows, RAG, routing, agents, tracing, and CrewAI-style agent teams.
recommendedBooks: Official textbook list to be updated; syllabus emphasizes practical LLM application engineering

overview: Applies AI to drug discovery with molecular representation, force fields, structure prediction, binding-site analysis, ADMET prediction, docking, virtual screening, molecular dynamics, and open-source AI/ML resources.
recommendedBooks: Noah Flynn, Machine Learning for Drug Discovery, Manning, 2024
Abhirup Khanna and May El Barachi, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Drug Discovery

overview: Applies machine learning to signal processing with audio and physiological signals, time-frequency representations, preprocessing, feature engineering, representation learning, multimodal learning, speech analysis, healthcare monitoring, and brain-computer interfaces.
recommendedBooks: Official textbook list to be updated; syllabus emphasizes applied ML for audio and physiological signals
